title: Fitting a domestic water softener bypass valve
subtitle: Install a three-way bypass valve assembly on the mains supply to allow a water softener to be serviced or removed without interrupting supply
excerpt: How to fit a bypass valve assembly for a domestic water softener. Covers cutting the mains supply pipe, fitting the bypass valve tee connections, and testing the three bypass positions (service, bypass, and off.
sourceNotes: UK water softener installation trade practice. Harvey water softeners and Kinetico generic bypass valve fitting guidance (referenced principles, not brand instructions). UK Water Supply (Water Fittings) Regulations 1999.

glossaryTerms.definition: A valve assembly, typically a three-valve or single-lever cartridge unit, that allows the incoming mains supply to be directed either through the water softener or directly to the outlet (bypassing the softener). In the bypass position, unsoftened water flows directly to the house with the softener isolated; in the service position, the softener is taken offline and both inlet and outlet are closed while the supply continues through the bypass line. Used to service the softener, replace resin, or remove the unit without interrupting the water supply.

content.text: lets you service or remove a water softener without cutting the mains supply. Most softeners come with a bypass block included. The plumbing job is connecting the copper supply pipe to the valve ports.

The bypass valve fits on the mains cold supply pipe after the main stopcock and before any branch supplies (typically under the kitchen sink or in a utility room. The softener should sit on a level surface within 1 metre of the valve connections. Note the flow direction marked on the valve body) inlet and outlet are not interchangeable.
Cut the supply pipe and fit the connections

Turn off the mains stopcock and drain the pipe by opening the nearest kitchen tap. Cut the mains supply pipe at the correct height for the bypass valve inlets. Fit compression or push-fit tee fittings on the cut ends to run the inlet and outlet connections to the bypass valve ports. For soldered connections: clean the pipe ends with emery, apply flux, slide the fittings on, and solder. Apply PTFE tape to all threaded connections on the valve ports and make up the connections hand-tight then one further quarter turn.

Restore the mains supply with the bypass valve in the bypass position (water flows direct, not through the softener. Check all connections for drips. Move the valve to the service position) all ports closed, softener isolated. Finally move to the in-service position: water flows through the softener. Check the softener connections for drips. Commission the softener according to the manufacturer's guide.
